blocks.content: The shouts of the medics were drowned out by the students' silence.
The sight of Aider's battered body on the stretcher spoke louder than any words.
The fate of the first person to challenge Shirone was the epitome of misery.
Fear was already taking root in the hearts of some students who had been weighing Shirone against Fermi.
With Aider out of the fight, Screamer's team was disqualified from the hill capture, but Shirone's expression was grim.
No matter how sweet the efficiency of evil, humans have a desire to like and do good.
At least, that's what we want to believe.
But Aider activated the AT algorithm at the last moment.
It wasn't as if a lightning bolt struck from the sky or a giant hand came down to snatch him away.

blocks.speaker: Aider
blocks.content: Are humans truly infinitely free beings? Or are they just part of a vast system beyond their perception?
Shirone's team wins. 20 points awarded. This concludes the hill capture evaluation.
Walking over to his friends, Shirone placed a hand on Maya's trembling shoulder and apologized.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I'm sorry we dragged you into such a tough fight.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'm fine. I'm more worried about you.
Shirone was a decisive and cold person, enough to completely remove Aider from the graduating class.
But that was just the manifestation of his insight, taking the shortest path to his goal, not strength born from emotion.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'It must be hard for you, Shirone...' In truth, he didn't want to fight anyone. All his troubles began from there.
What might be an easy choice for someone else was the most complex problem in the world for Shirone.
Aider received emergency treatment outside the battlefield.
When a medic confirmed his heartbeat and shouted, someone nearby ran off urgently.
Electric Monster Lycan, having seen enough, turned to Fermi.

blocks.speaker: Lycan
blocks.content: Aider succeeded. He really got under Shirone's skin.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: If he were someone who acted purely on emotion, he would be easy to manipulate. Though their natures were extremely different, Shirone was similar to him.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Actually, his emotions have only made him more composed. If he didn't crush Aider, a second or third Aider would appear. He calculated that it was best to nip it in the bud.

blocks.speaker: Lycan
blocks.content: Anyway, the strategy worked. We saw all of Shirone's techniques. Now we can gather spells that can counter him and strike back. Wasn't that the plan from the start?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Of course, but...
Fermi rested his chin in thought.
In an evaluation worth 20 points, anyone would give their best. But it's natural to want to save something like the laser-guided algorithm.
In that sense, Aider met the terms of the deal precisely. Yet, what was this nagging feeling in his mind?

blocks.speaker: Lycan
blocks.content: I understand.
Lycan turned back to Fermi.
He was hiding it? No, he never hid anything. Light Wings, Scatter Movement, Laser-Guided Algorithm. Even if it wasn't Aider, he would have used those cards if necessary.
So why was I mistaken? Because Shirone's growth rate is far faster than anyone else's.
Before joining the graduating class, he couldn't use any of these techniques. Now he had three new ones.
Even if we knew everything about Shirone now, predicting how much he would evolve by the graduation exam was impossible.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: In just three months, he mastered two intermediate and one advanced spell. In terms of speed, it's almost like the power enhancement I achieve through my financial management.
Without looking back, Fermi spoke to his companions.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Special bargain sale.

blocks.speaker: Sona's Hersy
blocks.content: Huh?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Tell all customers there's a 20% discount. And from now on, we only buy advanced spells. Buy as many as possible before the graduation exam.
Sona's Hersy approached with a worried expression.

blocks.speaker: Hersy
blocks.content: But Fermi, that will affect our profits this year...

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: What did I say?
Hersy's face turned pale under Fermi's gaze.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: If you don't eliminate minor variables, they become unmanageable when they hit.
As Hersy repeated the words, Fermi turned coldly.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Do it.

blocks.speaker: Hersy
blocks.content: Got it.
As Fermi left, the remaining four followed, glancing at each other.
The area around the battlefield was still chaotic.
Some students were desperately trying to calm Screamer.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Let go! I'll kill that bastard! Let go of me!

blocks.speaker: Medic
blocks.content: Screamer! Calm down! Aider is seriously injured! If you hit him, he'll die for real!
There might not be any place left to hit on someone with multiple fractures, but with Screamer's temper, nothing was certain.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: I'm hitting him to kill him! That arrogant bastard dared to ruin my plan?
Using an unagreed strategy had disqualified the team.
Plus, Aider's collusion with Fermi had driven Screamer, sensitive to back-alley loyalty, over the edge.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Ugh! This is so frustrating!
Screamer kicked the ground, throwing dirt at Aider.
A medic, unable to bear it, glared and pointed a finger at him.

blocks.speaker: Medic
blocks.content: You! Stay at least 10 meters away from the patient! Or I'll report you to the teacher!

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Damn it!
Regaining his senses, Screamer shook off the students' hands and turned away.
For now, there was nothing he could do, but once Aider recovered, he would have to be prepared to be half-dead.
Screamer walked to the water fountain, opened a bottle, and drank it all in one go.
When Shirone's group approached, he pointed at them with the bottle.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Don't get cocky just because you won. If it weren't for Aider, the outcome would have been different.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: That's funny! We had the game in the bag anyway. And you promised, didn't you? You said you'd apologize to Maya.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Tch! Apologize, my foot. Here, have some water.
Screamer handed over the bottle as if to end the matter.
But the reaction was unexpected.
Maya, still traumatized, flinched and trembled, making Screamer sigh.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: 'Ugh, scared after a few hits. How can someone like that become a magician...'
Maya had no talent for magic.
No, beyond talent, her mindset wasn't that of a magician.
People's personalities vary, and in reality, what matters is who gets their way.
Her swollen face, rabbit-like frightened eyes, and the way she held her hands like someone being punished were all irritating.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Maya, you...

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Just don't become a magician.
Screamer swallowed the words.
Seeing her as a person rather than a magician, he felt a bit of sympathy for her, looking dazed after being bitten by a mad dog.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Alright, I'm sorry.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: I'm sorry for calling it the sound of a pig being slaughtered. You sing well, so keep it up. Don't worry about Aider. I'll send him back to the hospital as soon as he returns.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Screamer...
To Maya, Screamer was a difficult person.
Always angry, often in the same group, making her heart race just by meeting his eyes.
Hearing his apology, all her pent-up sorrow burst out in tears.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Ugh, I'm leaving.
Screamer shook his head and left the training ground with another bottle.
ΓÇö Two weeks after the hill capture evaluation, on a holiday.
Shirone, Nade, and Iruki headed to the advanced class with flowers.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But is it okay for us to go? Graduating students rarely leave the Iron Gate. They say it creates a sense of disparity for the juniors.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: We should congratulate her. She took care of us a lot during the presentation.
As they entered the advanced class building, the eyes of the students in the hallway widened.
It was rare to see graduating seniors, let alone three at once.
Wow! It's Senior Shirone!
Students poured out of the classrooms, quickly filling the hallway.

blocks.speaker: Oern
blocks.content: Hello, seniors! I'm Oern, the top prospect of Class Four!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oern?

blocks.speaker: Oern
blocks.content: Yes. I enrolled this semester. You wouldn't know me, senior.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, a transfer student.

blocks.speaker: Oern
blocks.content: No. I started magic late too. Similar to you, Senior Shirone.
Shirone then took a closer look at Oern.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm?
Sensing a sudden dangerous aura, Shirone instinctively entered the Spirit Zone.
Oern's Spirit Zone pierced his chest like a sharp spear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, I see.
A promising student in the advanced class would surely have heard stories about Shirone.
Moreover, his followers, Mark and Maria, were well-established here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Aider's departure from the graduating class must have also influenced this.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Depending on the graduation schedule, there was a possibility that one student from Class Four could move up, so it was natural for Oern to be highly motivated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'How about this? You have to acknowledge me now, right?'
Oern knew how impressive the skills of the graduating class were.
However, he didn't think his talent was inferior to Shirone's, who was currently the most famous at the magic school.
Shirone had started in Class Seven despite entering at eighteen.
In contrast, Oern, two years younger, had entered directly into Class Four.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This was why graduating students rarely left the Iron Gate. If they had to deal with juniors who didn't understand the world, they wouldn't be able to get anything done.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'He certainly has talent. But...'
Suddenly, Shirone widened his eyes and glared at Oern.
His Spirit Zone transformed into an offensive mode, with about 200 spikes extending towards Oern.

blocks.speaker: Oern
blocks.content: Ah!
Startled, Oern fell to the ground.
He looked up at Shirone with a frightened expression.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You. You're specialized in offense, aren't you?

blocks.speaker: Oern
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, yes.
He had thought so.
Even from their brief encounter, Oern's energetic personality was evident, similar to Amy's.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sharpening your mind isn't everything. In offense, speed and agility are crucial. If you maintain the same mental state like you do, you'll quickly become predictable.

blocks.speaker: Oern
blocks.content: I-I see.
Oern looked up at Shirone in a daze.
The gap was much wider than he had vaguely imagined.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 'So this is the graduating class?'
As Shirone took a step forward, the students blocking the hallway parted to the sides like a river.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: He's really bold. Reminds me of when I first met Iruki.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, compared to Iruki, he's really humble.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Confidence is proportional to skill. In that sense, it's natural for that kid to be more humble than me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That endless confidence of yours...
As they continued their conversation, dozens of juniors watched the three of them walk away down the hallway.
